maximum_principle.conclusion: h_k(lambda)>=0 for every k>=1 and every finite lambda>=-100
maximum_principle.coordinate_regularity: Each z_k is C1 on compact heat intervals because A_k is positive analytic and the coefficient ODE is exact.
maximum_principle.dini_derivative: For m(t)<0, D_+m(t)=min{z_k'(t): z_k(t)=m(t)}>=0 because every active index is a negative spatial minimum.
maximum_principle.finite_active_set: Whenever m(t)<0, uniform c0-tail decay leaves only finitely many indices below m(t)/2; hence there is a finite active set locally in time.
maximum_principle.minimum_attainment: z_k->0 uniformly on compact time intervals, so every negative infimum is attained at finite k.
maximum_principle.negative_component_contradiction: On any connected component (alpha,beta) of {t:m(t)<0}, the Dini inequality makes m nondecreasing, contradicting m(alpha)=0.
maximum_principle.negative_minimum: If z_k is a negative spatial minimum, every term on the right is nonnegative.

proof_boundary: This artifact proves legitimate forward propagation of the full infinite ratio cone from lambda=-100 to every finite lambda>=-100, including lambda=0. It does not prove PF-infinity, the all-shape Jensen bridge, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

rows.claim: The positive zeta moment coefficients define a coordinatewise analytic heat trajectory on every finite real lambda interval.
rows.proof_boundary: Actual trajectory and coefficient ODE only.

rows.claim: Pointwise cone walls give uniformly vanishing adjacent defects and bounded coefficient ratios.
The adjacent defect equation is a one-sided cooperative transport with a nonnegative source.
The potential coefficient q_k has a uniform upper bound on each compact heat interval.
Uniform tail decay makes every negative spatial infimum attainable, and the transformed ODE points inward there.

diagnostics.conclusion: h_k(lambda)>=0 for every k>=1 and every finite lambda>=-100
diagnostics.coordinate_regularity: Each z_k is C1 on compact heat intervals because A_k is positive analytic and the coefficient ODE is exact.
diagnostics.dini_derivative: For m(t)<0, D_+m(t)=min{z_k'(t): z_k(t)=m(t)}>=0 because every active index is a negative spatial minimum.
diagnostics.finite_active_set: Whenever m(t)<0, uniform c0-tail decay leaves only finitely many indices below m(t)/2; hence there is a finite active set locally in time.
diagnostics.minimum_attainment: z_k->0 uniformly on compact time intervals, so every negative infimum is attained at finite k.
diagnostics.negative_component_contradiction: On any connected component (alpha,beta) of {t:m(t)<0}, the Dini inequality makes m nondecreasing, contradicting m(alpha)=0.
diagnostics.negative_minimum: If z_k is a negative spatial minimum, every term on the right is nonnegative.

rows.formula: h_k(-100)>=0 for all k => h_k(lambda)>=0 for all k and lambda>=-100
rows.proof_boundary: Infinite-index maximum principle for the adjacent wall only.

rows.claim: The exact pointwise walls and infinite maximum principle propagate the full ratio cone forward from lambda=-100.
The actual zeta coefficient sequence lies in the full infinite ratio cone at lambda=0.
rows.formula: full ratio cone at lambda=0 for every k>=1
rows.proof_boundary: Endpoint coefficient-ratio theorem; not PF-infinity or RH.

rows.claim: Convert the propagated ratio cone into the required all-shape Jensen-window or sign-regular theorem without circular endpoint assumptions.
rows.formula: propagated cone + missing structural bridge => Newman direction
rows.proof_boundary: Open all-order bridge; not RH or Lambda <= 0.

status: exact infinite ratio-cone propagation theorem and open all-order handoff
